attacking_style: Launches solo attacks from breakaways on sustained climbs at long range (115km out observed), targeting classification points rather than stage wins. Struggles to hold advantages when gradients exceed 15-20% in final kilometers.
team_tactics: Operates with significant independence in breakaways, relying on personal timing rather than visible team setup. Positioning awareness in peloton appears inconsistent when mass attacks occur.
terrain_preference: Sustained, moderate gradients for tempo-based climbing where he can distance breakaway companions. Exposed on explosive walls above 20% gradient and transitional/attacking moments requiring positional vigilance.
vulnerability: Explosive ultra-steep pitches (20%+) in finals completely neutralize earlier advantages. Poor anticipation of long-range attacks (30km out) suggests tactical blind spots in reading race dynamics.
key_patterns: Collapses on 20%+ gradients despite holding substantial gaps on moderate climbs (2 instances)
Attacks solo from breakaways on classified climbs at extreme distance to secure points
Caught out by positioning when teams launch transitional attacks outside traditional finale zones
Tempo climbing strength adequate for breakaway distancing but lacks final acceleration on walls
form_trajectory: negative: 2021 Vuelta showed tactical awareness gaps and physiological limitation on steep finals that negated earlier strength
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8
